title: tripogandra elongata
intro: Tripogandra elongata, also known as the "long tongue" or "long-tongued" plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Commelinaceae. It is native to Central and South America and has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of Tripogandra elongata include tea, infusion, and tincture, which can be used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
